title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: article demonstrates conservative cluster-permutation workflow gpbiometrics two-condition, one-dimensional time-course data. workflow intentionally narrow: participant-level time courses, two conditions, one temporal dimension, cluster mass based time-wise test statistics, permutation-based global inference, descriptive interpretation cluster timing. goal replace specialist EEG, MEG, pupillometry, mixed-model permutation toolboxes. goal provide transparent, reproducible, guarded workflow Gazepoint-derived time-course signals.

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: significant cluster-permutation result indicates evidence global null hypothesis condition difference anywhere tested time range, implemented permutation scheme. prove effect begins ends exactly reported cluster boundaries. Cluster timing described descriptive interval, precise physiological, attentional, cognitive onset offset.
code: library(gpbiometrics)

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: first create small synthetic two-condition time-course data set. example deliberately synthetic vignette can run without private participant data.
code: set.seed(123)  dat <- simulate_gazepoint_cluster_timecourse_data(   n_subjects = 12,   n_time = 60,   effect_start = 25,   effect_end = 38,   seed = 123 )  head(dat) #>   subject condition time true_effect       value #> 1     S01         A    1           0  0.02018967 #> 2     S02         A    1           0 -0.01327129 #> 3     S03         A    1           0  0.16734062 #> 4     S04         A    1           0  0.73239235 #> 5     S05         A    1           0  0.23146213 #> 6     S06         A    1           0 -0.35788062

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: Cluster-permutation tests require consistent participant--condition--time grid. audit helper checks whether expected cells present whether duplicate cells exist.
code: grid_audit <- audit_gazepoint_timecourse_grid(   data = dat,   subject = "subject",   condition = "condition",   time = "time",   value = "value" )  grid_audit #> Gazepoint time-course grid audit #> -------------------------------- #>  n_rows n_subjects n_conditions n_time_bins expected_cells #>    1440         12            2          60           1440 #>  observed_unique_cells missing_cells duplicate_cells missing_values #>                   1440             0               0              0 #>  complete_grid #>           TRUE #>  #> The grid is complete.

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: design diagnostic pre-analysis guardrail. checks whether data match current supported scope: two conditions, repeated participant observations, common time grid.
code: design_diagnostic <- diagnose_gazepoint_cluster_design(   data = dat,   subject = "subject",   condition = "condition",   time = "time",   value = "value",   design = "within" )  design_diagnostic #> Gazepoint cluster-permutation design diagnostic #> ------------------------------------------------ #> Design: within  #>  #>                              check passed severity #>                     two_conditions   TRUE       ok #>                      complete_grid   TRUE       ok #>                   minimum_subjects   TRUE       ok #>  within_subject_condition_presence   TRUE       ok #>        supported_by_current_runner   TRUE       ok #>                                                                                      message #>                                                          Exactly two conditions are present. #>                               Every participant-condition-time cell is present exactly once. #>                                                        At least 10 participants are present. #>                                                Every participant appears in every condition. #>  The current runner supports the diagnosed within-subject, two-condition time-course design. #>  #> No blocking design errors were detected.

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: preparation helper keeps analysis participant--condition--time level. raw trial-level samples supplied, repeated cells can aggregated cluster test.
code: prepared <- prepare_gazepoint_timecourse_test_data(   data = dat,   outcome_col = "value",   time_col = "time",   condition_col = "condition",   participant_col = "subject" )  head(prepared) #>   participant condition time       value #> 1         S01         A    1  0.02018967 #> 2         S01         A    2  0.09739217 #> 3         S01         A    3  0.04344428 #> 4         S01         A    4 -0.18120203 #> 5         S01         A    5 -0.23022769 #> 6         S01         A    6 -0.04747587

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: example uses small number permutations vignette builds quickly. real analyses, use permutations report exact settings.
code: cluster_result <- run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(   data = prepared,   outcome_col = "value",   time_col = "time",   condition_col = "condition",   participant_col = "participant",   design = "within",   condition_a = "A",   condition_b = "B",   n_permutations = 199,   cluster_forming_alpha = 0.05,   cluster_alpha = 0.05,   seed = 123 )  cluster_result #> Gazepoint cluster permutation test #> Design: within  #> Conditions: A - B  #> Participants: 12  #> Time points: 60  #> Permutations: 199  #> Cluster-forming alpha: 0.05  #> Cluster-level alpha: 0.05  #> Clusters: 3  #>  cluster_id direction start_time end_time n_timepoints      mass p_value #>           1  positive         53       53            1  2.647591   0.735 #>           2  negative         25       25            1  2.815247   0.655 #>           3  negative         27       38           12 40.347172   0.005 #>  significant #>        FALSE #>        FALSE #>         TRUE

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: cluster summary reports detected clusters, cluster mass, permutation p-values. time range useful description, interpreted exact onset offset.
code: cluster_summary <- summarize_gazepoint_time_clusters(cluster_result)  cluster_summary #>   cluster_id direction start_index end_index start_time end_time n_timepoints #> 1          1  positive          53        53         53       53            1 #> 2          2  negative          25        25         25       25            1 #> 3          3  negative          27        38         27       38           12 #>   signed_mass      mass p_value significant #> 1    2.647591  2.647591   0.735       FALSE #> 2   -2.815247  2.815247   0.655       FALSE #> 3  -40.347172 40.347172   0.005        TRUE

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: plotting helper overlays two time courses detected cluster information. Use plot communicate pattern, claim exact temporal localization.
code: plot_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(cluster_result)

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: null-distribution plot shows permutation distribution maximum cluster mass. helps users understand family-wise comparison used evaluate observed clusters.
code: plot_gazepoint_cluster_null_distribution(cluster_result)

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: Cluster-forming thresholds affect adjacent time points enter clusters. sensitivity check helps document whether conclusion highly dependent one arbitrary threshold.
code: threshold_sensitivity <- run_gazepoint_cluster_threshold_sensitivity(   data = prepared,   dv = "value",   time = "time",   condition = "condition",   subject = "participant",   thresholds = c(0.025, 0.05, 0.10),   cluster_alpha = 0.05,   seed = 123,   design = "within",   condition_a = "A",   condition_b = "B",   n_permutations = 99 )  threshold_sensitivity #> Gazepoint cluster-permutation threshold sensitivity #> ---------------------------------------------------- #>  threshold n_clusters min_p_value n_significant #>      0.025          6        0.01             3 #>      0.050          3        0.01             1 #>      0.100          2        0.01             1

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: reporting helper produces cautious wording. avoids treating cluster boundaries precise onsets offsets.
code: cluster_report <- report_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(cluster_result)  cluster_report #> The cluster-based permutation test indicated cluster-level evidence of a condition difference in the tested time course: cluster 3 (descriptive time range: 27 to 38, p = .005). The temporal extent of any detected cluster should be interpreted descriptively. The test evaluates evidence against the global null of no condition difference anywhere in the tested time range; it does not provide a precise estimate of effect onset, offset, latency, physiological event boundary, emotion, stress, cognition, diagnosis, or mechanism. Assumptions checked/reported: two-condition comparison; participant-level time courses; common participant-condition-time grid; permutation scheme matched to the supported design; cluster timing interpreted descriptively.

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: advanced function names exported deliberately guardrails. fail safely instead pretending provide validated ANOVA, mixed-model, TFCE, multidimensional, covariate-adjusted, parallel, onset/offset cluster inference.
code: try(run_gazepoint_tfce()) #> Error : `run_gazepoint_tfce()` is not implemented as a runnable inferential engine in gpbiometrics yet. #>  #> TFCE avoids a fixed cluster-forming threshold but introduces additional parameters and validation requirements. It should not be added until the fixed-threshold cluster workflow has been reviewed and validated. #>  #> The current validated scope is the conservative within-subject, two-condition, one-dimensional time-course workflow implemented in `run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation()`. #>  #> Recommended alternative: Use `run_gazepoint_cluster_threshold_sensitivity()` to inspect sensitivity to fixed cluster-forming thresholds.

title: Cluster-permutation workflow
text: Prefer wording : two-condition cluster-permutation test indicated evidence condition difference tested time course. reported cluster interval descriptive interpreted precise onset offset estimate. Avoid wording : effect began exactly time point X ended exactly time point Y.

title: gpbiometrics workflow
text: vignette gives compact workflow using gpbiometrics Gazepoint Biometrics Gazepoint GP3 biometric exports. package supports importing, schema checks, signal-quality auditing, preprocessing, feature extraction, TTL alignment, model-ready table preparation, visual diagnostics, reproducible report generation. examples evaluated vignette build require local Gazepoint export files.

title: gpbiometrics workflow
code: library(gpbiometrics)

title: gpbiometrics workflow
text: package includes public synthetic Gazepoint-like demo dataset. simulates public-service touchscreen kiosk usability task 36 synthetic participants four tasks per participant. design crosses interface complexity (simple vs. dense) feedback clarity (clear vs. ambiguous). files contain gaze, AOI labels, pupil diameter, GSR/EDA, HR, IBI, pulse waveform, engagement dial, TTL markers, task metadata. dataset fully artificial provided examples, tests, vignettes, software demonstration. real participant physiology.
code: demo_dir <- system.file(   "extdata",   "gazepoint_biometrics_kiosk_demo_exports",   package = "gpbiometrics" )  workflow <- run_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(   path = demo_dir,   include_all_gaze = TRUE,   include_fixations = FALSE,   include_other_csv = FALSE,   expected_sampling_rate_hz = 60 )  summarise_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(workflow)

text: data data frame containing biometric samples. ttl_cols Optional TTL marker columns. "rising" keeps inactive--active transitions, "change" keeps changes among active event values, "active" keeps every active sample. pre_window_ms, post_window_ms Event window milliseconds usable time column available. pre_window_samples, post_window_samples Event window samples usable time column available. omitted, event sample kept. collapse_nearby_ms Optional minimum distance retained events within group, milliseconds. require_valid_ttl TRUE, detected TTL validity column must active row count event.

title: Create a Gazepoint Biometrics report — create_gazepoint_biometrics_report
text: Creates structured, manuscript-oriented report object Gazepoint Biometrics data, workflow outputs, quality checks, report tables, methods text, reporting checklists. report intentionally conservative: GSR/EDA described electrodermal activity/arousal-related signal rather emotional valence; heart-rate interpretation tied baseline/task context; eye-tracking described visual attention rather direct cognition; raw HRV columns treated HRV metrics.

what: gpbiometrics: Process, Audit, Plot, and Report Gazepoint Biometrics Data — gpbiometrics-package
title: gpbiometrics: Process, Audit, Plot, and Report Gazepoint Biometrics Data — gpbiometrics-package
text: gpbiometrics provides helper functions importing, checking, preprocessing, summarising, diagnosing, plotting, reporting Gazepoint Biometrics exports. designed reproducible offline workflows involving physiological, response-channel, event-marker, timing data exported alongside Gazepoint eye-tracking recordings.

title: gpbiometrics: Process, Audit, Plot, and Report Gazepoint Biometrics Data — gpbiometrics-package
text: package supports common Gazepoint biometric channels, including GSR/EDA, heart rate, interbeat interval RR-style intervals, engagement dial, TTL markers. provides functions folder-level import, data-summary import, schema timebase detection, active-channel detection, column validation, missingness audits, signal-quality audits, sampling timing audits, dropout flatline flagging, baseline correction, smoothing, window summaries, TTL event extraction, biometric--gaze synchronisation, exclusion recommendations, workflow diagnostics, report-ready tables, cautious methods text, lightweight report generation, base-R diagnostic plotting. Schema timing helpers include standardise_gazepoint_biometric_names(), detect_gazepoint_biometric_schema(), detect_gazepoint_time_columns(), detect_gazepoint_biometric_timebase(). Signal-availability quality helpers include summarise_gazepoint_biometric_validity(), flag_gazepoint_biometric_dropouts(), audit_gazepoint_gsr_quality(), audit_gazepoint_hr_quality(), audit_gazepoint_engagement_dial(), audit_gazepoint_ibi_quality(). Preprocessing summary helpers include baseline_correct_gazepoint_gsr(), baseline_correct_gazepoint_hr(), smooth_gazepoint_biometrics(), convert_gazepoint_gsr_to_conductance(), summarise_gazepoint_gsr_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_hr_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_engagement_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_dial_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_ibi_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_gsr_tonic_phasic(), summarise_gazepoint_multimodal_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_ibi_hrv_windows(), summarise_gazepoint_full_biometric_windows(). Synchronisation, modelling, workflow, reporting helpers include sync_gazepoint_biometrics_with_gaze(), join_gazepoint_biometrics_to_master(), join_gazepoint_biometrics_to_gp3tools(), prepare_gazepoint_multimodal_model_data(), run_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(), summarise_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(), diagnose_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(), create_gazepoint_biometrics_checklist(), create_gazepoint_biometrics_methods_text(), create_gazepoint_biometrics_report_tables(), write_gazepoint_biometrics_report_tables(), create_gazepoint_biometrics_report(). Diagnostic plotting helpers include plot_gazepoint_biometric_signals() plot_gazepoint_biometric_quality(). package treats biometric signals conservatively. GSR/EDA handled electrodermal activity arousal-related signal rather emotional valence. Heart-rate summaries interpreted relative baseline, artefact handling, task context. Raw Gazepoint HRV columns treated validity vendor flags unless independent documentation proves otherwise. IBI-derived HRV-style summaries computed genuine interbeat interval RR-style interval columns. Eye-tracking measures, combined biometric data, interpreted indicators visual attention rather direct evidence cognition, scrutiny, evaluation.

NULL, numeric columns except time grouping columns used. time_col Optional time column interpolation. group_cols Optional grouping columns. Imputation performed within groups. max_gap Maximum missing-gap length, samples, impute. Longer gaps remain missing. Defaults Inf. fill_edges TRUE, leading trailing gaps filled using nearest observed value methods support . constant_value Value used method = "constant". add_flags TRUE data data frame, add logical <column>_was_imputed columns. treat_infinite_as_missing TRUE, infinite values treated missing imputation.

title: Summarise Gazepoint SCR responses in event windows — summarise_gazepoint_scr_event_windows
text: data Optional Gazepoint biometric data frame. Required events NULL events derived TTL columns. scr_peaks gazepoint_scr_peak_detection object returned detect_gazepoint_scr_peaks(), data frame detected peaks. events Optional event data frame. NULL, events derived TTL columns data. time_col Optional time/counter column data. Used TTL-derived events. event_time_col Optional event-time column events. NULL, common event-time column names detected. event_id_col Optional event identifier column events. event_label_col Optional event label/condition column events. group_cols Optional grouping columns used match events peaks. ttl_cols Optional TTL marker columns used events = NULL. ttl_valid_col Optional TTL validity column. supplied, TTL-derived events require column non-zero. event_detection Event-detection rule TTL columns. "rising" detects rising edges; "active" treats every active TTL row event. analysis_window Numeric length-two vector giving event-relative analysis window units time_col event_time_col. response_window Numeric length-two vector giving event-relative response window used binary SCR response flag. amplitude_col Column peak table containing SCR amplitude. peak_time_col Column peak table containing peak time. onset_time_col Column peak table containing onset time. rise_time_col Column peak table containing SCR rise time. recovery_time_col Column peak table containing recovery time peak. peak_status_col Column peak table containing peak status. peak_selection choose one peak several peaks fall response window. "largest_amplitude" selects largest response; "first_peak" selects earliest peak. collapse_simultaneous_events Logical. TRUE, events group event time collapsed one row matching peaks. useful Gazepoint TTL0–TTL6 channels mark event simultaneously.

title: gpbiometrics roadmap status
text: Version 0.2.0 appropriate package now includes substantially expanded exported API, roadmap-closure helpers, compatibility aliases, tests, documentation, NEWS entries, pkgdown pages.

title: gpbiometrics 0.2.0.9000
text: Added advanced cluster-permutation guardrails external export helpers. guardrail functions explicitly prevent unsupported ANOVA, mixed-model, TFCE, multidimensional, covariate-adjusted, parallel, onset/offset cluster claims directing users back validated two-condition time-course workflow. Added export helpers MNE-, permuco-, permutes-style external workflows. Added cluster-permutation diagnostics, reporting, sensitivity, simulation, null-distribution plotting, export helpers: audit_gazepoint_timecourse_grid(), diagnose_gazepoint_cluster_design(), plot_gazepoint_cluster_null_distribution(), report_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(), run_gazepoint_cluster_threshold_sensitivity(), simulate_gazepoint_cluster_timecourse_data(), export_gazepoint_cluster_results().

title: gpbiometrics 0.2.0.9000
text: Added experimental within-subject, two-condition cluster-based permutation prototype Gazepoint-derived time-course signals. Added prepare_gazepoint_timecourse_test_data(), run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(), summarize_gazepoint_time_clusters(), plot_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(). Added experimental pkgdown article describing prototype emphasizing cluster timing interpreted descriptively rather precise onset offset evidence.

title: gpbiometrics 0.2.0
text: Added roadmap compatibility aliases discoverability wrappers column standardization, format validation, pupil cleaning/interpolation, PPG-derived respiration estimation, mixed-model data preparation. Added compare_gazepoint_conditions_bootstrap() lightweight percentile-bootstrap condition comparisons Gazepoint-derived trial-level, event-locked, participant-level outcomes. Added physiology/QC refinement helpers HRV segment quality flags, SCR latency metrics, pairwise multimodal signal-lag screening, exploratory PPG-derived respiration-rate estimation. Added alignment, AOI time-course, event-locked synthesis, dashboard helpers event-based stream alignment, binned AOI proportions, multimodal event-locked summaries, compact quality-dashboard exports. Added front-door audit missingness helpers: unified Gazepoint biometrics preflight audit, dedicated missingness/gap summaries, generic signal detrending slow drift biometric pupil signals. Added exact roadmap backlog helpers schema standardization, export schema auditing, multimodal simulation, sampling-irregularity QC, sync-drift diagnostics, AOI dwell summaries, scanpath metrics, analysis manifests, PPG template-similarity QC, Haar-style HRV wavelet PSD summaries. Added remaining roadmap helpers SCR habituation/recovery, event-related pupil summaries, tracking ratios, pupil-luminance audits, PPG morphology, segment-level PPG quality, generic event-log import, event--biometric matching, column validation, reproducibility metadata. Added import_gazepoint_data() single-entry helper importing Gazepoint session folders named lists data frames. Added impute_gazepoint_missing() CRAN-safe interpolation short missing gaps continuous Gazepoint signals. Added pupil gaze helpers: detect_gazepoint_pupil_blinks(), clean_gazepoint_pupil_signal(), filter_gazepoint_gaze(), summarize_gazepoint_fixations(). Added event-level physiology helpers: epoch_gazepoint_scr(), normalize_gazepoint_scr(), flag_gazepoint_rr_outliers(), compute_gazepoint_engagement_index(). Added workflow helpers modeling reporting: create_gazepoint_trial_regressors(), report_gazepoint_data_quality(), preprocess_gazepoint_all(). Added simulate_gazepoint_eye_data() synthetic Gazepoint-style gaze, fixation, pupil, blink, validity data teaching, tests, vignettes, smoke tests. Updated package metadata, README, pkgdown configuration reflect expanded Gazepoint-native workflow surface.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.1
text: Added Gazepoint-native pyHRV-style HRV workflows, including time-domain, frequency-domain, nonlinear, Poincare, sample-entropy, DFA, PSD, tachogram, radar-chart, export/import, --one HRV helpers. Added BioSPPy-style Gazepoint biosignal workflows EDA event extraction, EDA recovery-time estimation, PPG/BVP processing, PPG pulse templates, PPG onset detection, local RRI artifact correction, RRI detrending, power spectra, band power, phase locking, signal correlation. Added PsPM-style Gazepoint preprocessing modelling workflows marker extraction, marker-channel combination, trimming, session splitting, recording merging, SCR preprocessing/QC, event-centred segment extraction, convolution-GLM design creation, GLM fitting, model-estimate export. Extended HeartPy-style PPG support segmentwise processing, signal scaling, filtering, smoothing, clipping reconstruction, binary-quality checks, breathing-rate visualisation, Poincare plotting, frequency measures. Updated package metadata README describe new Gazepoint-native toolbox-style workflow layers without claiming exact external-toolbox equivalence.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-04 Added HeartPy-style Gazepoint pulse/PPG workflows, including input preparation, clipping reconstruction, peak enhancement, Butterworth-style filtering, Hampel correction, adaptive peak detection, peak rejection, HR/IBI-style measures, breathing-rate estimation, plotting, report-table generation, optional Python HeartPy cross-checking reticulate.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Initial validated development release gpbiometrics, R package importing, validating, quality-checking, preprocessing, synchronising, summarising, modelling, plotting, reporting Gazepoint Biometrics Gazepoint GP3 biometric exports. package focuses Gazepoint-specific biometric channels, including GSR/EDA, heart rate, interbeat intervals, pulse signal, engagement dial, TTL markers, pupil-related columns, AOI fields, synchronisation variables. current feature inventory contains 155 available user-facing helpers across 11 complete workflow domains. Interpretation intentionally conservative: biometric features treated physiological descriptors, quality-control outputs, analysis-ready signals, direct labels emotion, stress, cognition, preference, health status, diagnosis.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added import helpers single files export folders, including import_gazepoint_biometrics(), import_gazepoint_biometric_folder(), import_gazepoint_data_summary(), import_gazepoint_lsl_xdf(). Added Gazepoint schema channel-detection helpers, including check_gazepoint_biometric_columns(), detect_gazepoint_biometric_schema(), detect_gazepoint_time_columns(), detect_active_biometric_channels(), standardise_gazepoint_biometric_names(). Added main workflow wrapper run_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow() summary/diagnostic helpers, including summarise_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow() diagnose_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(). Added synthetic data generation simulate_gazepoint_biometrics() examples, teaching, controlled validation.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added validation, missingness, sampling, signal-activity, time-reset, dropout, distributional-drift, real-data readiness checks. Added run_gazepoint_biometrics_real_data_readiness() final readiness gate real Gazepoint exports. Added exclusion-recommendation helpers participant-level window-level biometric quality decisions. Added artifact-detection helpers, including MAD-based, Kleckner-style, SVM-feature workflows. Added audit_gazepoint_gsr_units() help distinguish conductance-like resistance-like GSR columns downstream EDA/SCR processing. Added audit_gazepoint_stabilization_period() flagging trimming initial electrode-stabilisation period.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added baseline correction, smoothing, within-unit standardisation, z-score/range correction, adaptive EMA smoothing, wavelet denoising, quantisation-noise handling, optional autoencoder-denoising bridges. Added EDA/GSR unit auditing conductance-conversion helpers. Added environmental stimulus-confound controls, including correct_gazepoint_eda_temperature(), audit_gazepoint_stabilization_period(), regress_gazepoint_pupil_luminance(). Added British American spelling aliases useful, including standardise/standardize variants.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added EDA/GSR quality audits, tonic/phasic summaries, SCR event peak detection, SCR event-window summaries, nonresponder screening, threshold-sensitivity checks, SCR multiverse workflows. Added SCR recovery-time extraction extract_gazepoint_scr_recovery_times(), including half-recovery 63 percent recovery-time summaries. Added advanced EDA helpers spectral power, complexity, TVSymp-style analysis, bilateral EDA asymmetry, skin-potential analysis, AC admittance/susceptance, stochastic change-point screening, EDA-gram-style visualisation. Added external EDA interoperability helpers Ledalab, PsPM, cvxEDA, NeuroKit-style input, DCM/CTSI-oriented bridges. Added run_gazepoint_automated_statistics() exploratory group comparisons normality screening, ANOVA/Kruskal-Wallis selection, post-hoc testing, multiplicity correction.

what: Pulse, IBI, HR, HRV, and respiration
title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added HR, IBI, HRV quality consistency checks. Added HR/IBI window summaries IBI-derived HRV feature extraction. Added nonlinear geometric HRV descriptors, including RQA, fragmentation, asymmetry, FuzzyEn/CSI, RCMSE, surrogate nonlinearity testing, IPFM-style impulse-train modelling. Added Gazepoint pulse beat-candidate extraction extract_gazepoint_beats_kmeans(). Added respiration-related helpers, including PPG-derived respiration, ECG-derived respiration PCA bridges, CEEMDAN-style respiration extraction, RSA proxy calculation, Kalman fusion respiration proxy streams. Added point-process cardiorespiratory directionality helpers advanced exploratory analysis.

what: TTL, synchronisation, windows, and model-ready data

Added multimodal time-window summaries model-ready table preparation helpers biometric, AOI-linked, LME-style analyses. Added chunking online design-optimisation decision-support helpers advanced experimental workflows. Added helpers synchronising Gazepoint Biometrics outputs Gazepoint eye-tracking master tables.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added AOI-linked biometric summaries, AOI-biometric model data preparation, AOI-biometric plotting. Added biometric signal plots, quality plots, decomposition plots, SCR plots, multimodal timelines, activity/time-reset plots, report dashboards, SCR specification-curve plots, saccade main-sequence plots, EDA-gram-style plots. Added plot-contract helpers store plot data, settings, interpretation metadata reproducibility.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: Added checklist, methods-text, report-table, report-bundle, preregistration-template, Shiny/annotator helpers. Added create_gazepoint_biometrics_feature_inventory() programmatic workflow coverage checks. Added formatted inventory helpers, format_gazepoint_biometrics_feature_inventory() summarise_gazepoint_biometrics_feature_inventory(). Added compact user-facing README first workflow vignette, vignettes/gpbiometrics-workflow.Rmd. Updated workflow documentation use current run_gazepoint_biometrics_workflow(path = ...) API export report bundles export_gazepoint_biometrics_report_bundle(). Added public, fully synthetic Gazepoint-like kiosk demo dataset inst/extdata/gazepoint_biometrics_kiosk_demo_exports/. demo dataset contains 36 synthetic participants, four kiosk tasks per participant, 69,120 rows, 36 -gaze CSV exports, task metadata, gaze/AOI fields, pupil columns, GSR/EDA, HR, IBI, pulse waveform, engagement dial, TTL markers. Added data-raw/create_gazepoint_biometrics_kiosk_demo_exports.R regenerate synthetic demo exports reproducibly. Added package tests ensure synthetic kiosk demo remains available, importable, schema-valid.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0

Advanced bridge functions prepare structure data external workflows unless explicit cross-check execution requested available.

title: gpbiometrics 0.1.0
text: EDA/GSR/SCR features describe electrodermal dynamics arousal-related physiology; directly infer emotion, stress, cognition, health status, diagnosis. HR, IBI, HRV, pulse, respiration-proxy features describe cardiovascular signal-derived dynamics; clinical labels. Pupil outputs affected luminance visual context; luminance-adjusted residuals proof cognitive-load-effects. AOI-linked biometric summaries describe signal values AOI exposure establish emotional valence, preference, cognitive evaluation . Automated statistics advanced models exploratory/reporting aids unless matched preregistered design reviewed analytically.
